headline: DEVELOPING: IRGC claims Wave 84 destroyed US refueling fleet at Al-Kharj base — satellite images show damaged KC-135s — Pentagon has not confirmed or denied
summary: IRGC says its 84th wave struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, claiming destruction of US aerial refueling tanker fleet. Satellite imagery appears to show at least one KC-135 destroyed and several damaged. Military Times reports 10 US troops wounded. Trump says tankers suffered 'virtually no damage.' Pentagon has not released a detailed response.
tickerText: DEVELOPING: IRGC Wave 84 claims destruction of US refueling fleet at Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj); satellite images appear to show KC-135 Stratotankers damaged or destroyed; Military Times: 10 US troops wounded, 2 seriously; Trump: tankers suffered 'virtually no damage'; IRGC commander warns response will go 'beyond eye for eye'; IRGC turns back 3 ships at Hormuz including 2 Chinese-flagged vessels

items.label: Wave 84 — Joint missile and drone operation on Al-Kharj
items.detail: IRGC Aerospace Force and Navy say they conducted a 'special joint missile and drone operation' targeting US aerial refueling tanker aircraft at Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj), Saudi Arabia. Claims they penetrated base air defenses with solid- and liquid-fueled missiles and destroyed multiple wide-body and heavy support aircraft.

items.label: IRGC says 3 KC-135 Stratotankers completely destroyed
items.detail: Iranian-linked accounts claim 3 KC-135 refueling aircraft were completely destroyed in the strike, along with damage to anti-missile defense systems at the base.

items.label: IRGC commander: 'Beyond eye for eye — just wait'
items.detail: BG Seyyed Majid Mousavi (IRGC Aerospace Force commander) warned retaliation will escalate beyond proportional response after US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants. Ordered evacuation of workers at all US/Israeli-linked industrial sites across the Gulf.

items.label: Military Times: 10 US troops wounded, 2 seriously
items.detail: Military Times reports approximately 10 US service members were wounded in the Al-Kharj attack, with 2 in serious condition. Pentagon has not released a detailed official response to IRGC's specific claims.

items.label: Trump: tankers suffered 'virtually no damage'
items.detail: President Trump claimed the tankers at the Saudi base suffered 'virtually no damage,' contradicting both IRGC claims and some satellite analysis.

items.label: Satellite images appear to show KC-135 destroyed
items.detail: The Aviationist and Defence Security Asia analyzed satellite imagery that appears to show at least 1 KC-135 Stratotanker completely destroyed (visible charring, missing engines, collapsed structures) and several others heavily damaged. An E-3 Sentry AWACS plane was also reportedly damaged. Western analysts describe approximately 5 aircraft damaged total.

items.label: Defence Security Asia satellite analysis
items.detail: Satellite images analyzed by Defence Security Asia show damage consistent with at least 3 KC-135 tankers being hit at the base.

items.label: 3 ships turned back at Strait of Hormuz
items.detail: IRGC Navy turned away 3 ships attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz on March 27: two Chinese-flagged COSCO container vessels (CSCL Indian Ocean and CSCL Arctic Ocean) and the Marshall Islands-flagged Lotus Rising. IRGC opened a 'tolled passage' system requiring ships to submit details to approved intermediaries and pay in yuan.
